authors_text: Chaojin Qing,Qing Ye,Bin Cai,Wenhui Liu,Jiafan Wang
title: Deep Learning for 1-Bit Compressed Sensing-based Superimposed CSI Feedback
title_zh: 基于 1 位压缩感知的叠加 CSI 反馈的深度学习
abstract: In frequency-division duplexing (FDD) massive multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) systems, 1-bit compressed sensing (CS)-based superimposed channel state information (CSI) feedback has shown many advantages, while still faces many challenges, such as low accuracy of the downlink CSI recovery and large processing delays. To overcome these drawbacks, this paper proposes a deep learning (DL) scheme to improve the 1-bit compressed sensing-based superimposed CSI feedback. On the user side, the downlink CSI is compressed with the 1-bit CS technique, superimposed on the uplink user data sequences (UL-US), and then sent back to the base station (BS). At the BS, based on the model-driven approach and assisted by the superimposition-interference cancellation technology, a multi-task detection network is first constructed for detecting both the UL-US and downlink CSI. In particular, this detection network is jointly trained to detect the UL-US and downlink CSI simultaneously, capturing a globally optimized network parameter. Then, with the recovered bits for the downlink CSI, a lightweight reconstruction scheme, which consists of an initial feature extraction of the downlink CSI with the simplified traditional method and a single hidden layer network, is utilized to reconstruct the downlink CSI with low processing delay. Compared with the 1-bit CS-based superimposed CSI feedback scheme, the proposed scheme improves the recovery accuracy of the UL-US and downlink CSI with lower processing delay and possesses robustness against parameter variations.
summary: In frequency-division duplexing (FDD) massive multiple-input multiple-output (MIMO) systems, 1-bit compressed sensing (CS)-based superimposed channel state information (CSI) feedback has shown many advantages, while still faces many challenges, such as low accuracy of the downlink CSI recovery and large processing delays. To overcome these drawbacks, this paper proposes a deep learning (DL) scheme to improve the 1-bit compressed sensing-based superimposed CSI feedback. On the user side, the downlink CSI is compressed with the 1-bit CS technique, superimposed on the uplink user data sequences (UL-US), and then sent back to the base station (BS). At the BS, based on the model-driven approach and assisted by the superimposition-interference cancellation technology, a multi-task detection network is first constructed for detecting both the UL-US and downlink CSI. In particular, this detection network is jointly trained to detect the UL-US and downlink CSI simultaneously, capturing a globally optimized network parameter. Then, with the recovered bits for the downlink CSI, a lightweight reconstruction scheme, which consists of an initial feature extraction of the downlink CSI with the simplified traditional method and a single hidden layer network, is utilized to reconstruct the downlink CSI with low processing delay. Compared with the 1-bit CS-based superimposed CSI feedback scheme, the proposed scheme improves the recovery accuracy of the UL-US and downlink CSI with lower processing delay and possesses robustness against parameter variations.
abstract_zh: 在频分双工（FDD）大规模多输入多输出（MIMO）系统中，基于1比特压缩感知（CS）的叠加信道状态信息（CSI）反馈显示出许多优势，但仍面临下行CSI恢复精度低、处理延迟大等挑战。为了克服这些缺点，本文提出了一种深度学习（DL）方案来改进基于 1 位压缩感知的叠加 CSI 反馈。在用户侧，下行CSI采用1比特CS技术压缩，叠加在上行用户数据序列（UL-US）上，然后发送回基站（BS）。在BS处，基于模型驱动方法并辅以叠加干扰消除技术，首先构建多任务检测网络来检测UL-US和下行CSI。特别是，该检测网络经过联合训练，可以同时检测 UL-US 和下行链路 CSI，捕获全局优化的网络参数。然后，利用恢复的下行CSI比特，利用简化的传统方法和单隐层网络对下行CSI进行初始特征提取的轻量级重建方案，以低处理延迟重建下行CSI。与基于1比特CS的叠加CSI反馈方案相比，该方案提高了UL-US和下行CSI的恢复精度，处理延迟更低，并且对参数变化具有鲁棒性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于深度学习的1位压缩感知叠加CSI反馈方案，以提高频分双工大规模MIMO系统中CSI恢复的准确性并降低处理延迟。
AI.motivation: 现有的1位压缩感知叠加CSI反馈方案存在下行CSI恢复精度低、处理延迟大等问题，需改进以提升系统性能。
AI.method: 在用户端采用1位压缩感知技术压缩下行CSI并叠加至上行用户数据序列后反馈；在基站端构建多任务检测网络联合检测上行数据和CSI，并通过轻量级重构方案实现低延迟CSI恢复。
AI.result: 相较传统方案，所提方法在提升上行数据和下行CSI恢复精度的同时降低了处理延迟，并对参数变化具有鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: 深度学习方法有效解决了1位压缩感知叠加CSI反馈中的关键挑战，为FDD大规模MIMO系统提供了高效可靠的解决方案。

authors_text: Fa-Ting Hong,Longhao Zhang,Li Shen,Dan Xu
title: Depth-Aware Generative Adversarial Network for Talking Head Video Generation
title_zh: 用于生成头像视频的深度感知生成对抗网络
abstract: Talking head video generation aims to produce a synthetic human face video that contains the identity and pose information respectively from a given source image and a driving video.Existing works for this task heavily rely on 2D representations (e.g. appearance and motion) learned from the input images. However, dense 3D facial geometry (e.g. pixel-wise depth) is extremely important for this task as it is particularly beneficial for us to essentially generate accurate 3D face structures and distinguish noisy information from the possibly cluttered background. Nevertheless, dense 3D geometry annotations are prohibitively costly for videos and are typically not available for this video generation task. In this paper, we first introduce a self-supervised geometry learning method to automatically recover the dense 3D geometry (i.e.depth) from the face videos without the requirement of any expensive 3D annotation data. Based on the learned dense depth maps, we further propose to leverage them to estimate sparse facial keypoints that capture the critical movement of the human head. In a more dense way, the depth is also utilized to learn 3D-aware cross-modal (i.e. appearance and depth) attention to guide the generation of motion fields for warping source image representations. All these contributions compose a novel depth-aware generative adversarial network (DaGAN) for talking head generation. Extensive experiments conducted demonstrate that our proposed method can generate highly realistic faces, and achieve significant results on the unseen human faces.
summary: Talking head video generation aims to produce a synthetic human face video that contains the identity and pose information respectively from a given source image and a driving video.Existing works for this task heavily rely on 2D representations (e.g. appearance and motion) learned from the input images. However, dense 3D facial geometry (e.g. pixel-wise depth) is extremely important for this task as it is particularly beneficial for us to essentially generate accurate 3D face structures and distinguish noisy information from the possibly cluttered background. Nevertheless, dense 3D geometry annotations are prohibitively costly for videos and are typically not available for this video generation task. In this paper, we first introduce a self-supervised geometry learning method to automatically recover the dense 3D geometry (i.e.depth) from the face videos without the requirement of any expensive 3D annotation data. Based on the learned dense depth maps, we further propose to leverage them to estimate sparse facial keypoints that capture the critical movement of the human head. In a more dense way, the depth is also utilized to learn 3D-aware cross-modal (i.e. appearance and depth) attention to guide the generation of motion fields for warping source image representations. All these contributions compose a novel depth-aware generative adversarial network (DaGAN) for talking head generation. Extensive experiments conducted demonstrate that our proposed method can generate highly realistic faces, and achieve significant results on the unseen human faces.
abstract_zh: 会说话的头部视频生成旨在生成一个合成人脸视频，其中分别包含来自给定源图像和驾驶视频的身份和姿势信息。该任务的现有工作严重依赖于从输入图像中学习的 2D 表示（例如外观和运动）。然而，密集的 3D 面部几何结构（例如像素级深度）对于这项任务极其重要，因为它对于我们从本质上生成准确的 3D 面部结构并从可能杂乱的背景中区分噪声信息特别有益。然而，密集的 3D 几何注释对于视频来说成本高昂，并且通常不适用于此视频生成任务。在本文中，我们首先介绍了一种自监督几何学习方法，可以自动从面部视频中恢复密集的 3D 几何（即深度），而不需要任何昂贵的 3D 注释数据。基于学习到的密集深度图，我们进一步建议利用它们来估计捕捉人类头部关键运动的稀疏面部关键点。以更密集的方式，深度还用于学习 3D 感知跨模式（即外观和深度）注意力，以指导用于扭曲源图像表示的运动场的生成。所有这些贡献组成了一个新颖的用于头部说话的深度感知生成对抗网络（DaGAN）。大量的实验表明，我们提出的方法可以生成高度逼真的人脸，并在看不见的人脸上取得显着的结果。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度感知的生成对抗网络（DaGAN），用于生成具有身份和姿态信息的 talking head 视频。
AI.motivation: 现有方法依赖2D表示生成 talking head 视频，但缺乏密集3D几何信息（如深度），导致生成效果受限。而深度信息对准确生成3D面部结构和区分背景噪声至关重要，但获取成本极高。
AI.method: 提出自监督几何学习方法恢复视频中的密集3D深度，利用深度图估计面部关键点，并引入3D感知的跨模态注意力机制，指导运动场生成，最终构建DaGAN模型。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法能生成高度逼真的 talking head 视频，在未见人脸数据上取得显著效果。
AI.conclusion: 深度信息对 talking head 视频生成至关重要，DaGAN 通过自监督学习有效利用深度数据，为该领域提供了新思路。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种基于深度感知的生成对抗网络（DaGAN），用于生成具有身份和姿态信息的 talking head 视频。

search_text: Depth-Aware Generative Adversarial Network for Talking Head Video Generation 用于生成头像视频的深度感知生成对抗网络 Talking head video generation aims to produce a synthetic human face video that contains the identity and pose information respectively from a given source image and a driving video.Existing works for this task heavily rely on 2D representations (e.g. appearance and motion) learned from the input images. However, dense 3D facial geometry (e.g. pixel-wise depth) is extremely important for this task as it is particularly beneficial for us to essentially generate accurate 3D face structures and distinguish noisy information from the possibly cluttered background. Nevertheless, dense 3D geometry annotations are prohibitively costly for videos and are typically not available for this video generation task. In this paper, we first introduce a self-supervised geometry learning method to automatically recover the dense 3D geometry (i.e.depth) from the face videos without the requirement of any expensive 3D annotation data. Based on the learned dense depth maps, we further propose to leverage them to estimate sparse facial keypoints that capture the critical movement of the human head. In a more dense way, the depth is also utilized to learn 3D-aware cross-modal (i.e. appearance and depth) attention to guide the generation of motion fields for warping source image representations. All these contributions compose a novel depth-aware generative adversarial network (DaGAN) for talking head generation. Extensive experiments conducted demonstrate that our proposed method can generate highly realistic faces, and achieve significant results on the unseen human faces. 会说话的头部视频生成旨在生成一个合成人脸视频，其中分别包含来自给定源图像和驾驶视频的身份和姿势信息。该任务的现有工作严重依赖于从输入图像中学习的 2D 表示（例如外观和运动）。然而，密集的 3D 面部几何结构（例如像素级深度）对于这项任务极其重要，因为它对于我们从本质上生成准确的 3D 面部结构并从可能杂乱的背景中区分噪声信息特别有益。然而，密集的 3D 几何注释对于视频来说成本高昂，并且通常不适用于此视频生成任务。在本文中，我们首先介绍了一种自监督几何学习方法，可以自动从面部视频中恢复密集的 3D 几何（即深度），而不需要任何昂贵的 3D 注释数据。基于学习到的密集深度图，我们进一步建议利用它们来估计捕捉人类头部关键运动的稀疏面部关键点。以更密集的方式，深度还用于学习 3D 感知跨模式（即外观和深度）注意力，以指导用于扭曲源图像表示的运动场的生成。所有这些贡献组成了一个新颖的用于头部说话的深度感知生成对抗网络（DaGAN）。大量的实验表明，我们提出的方法可以生成高度逼真的人脸，并在看不见的人脸上取得显着的结果。 本文提出一种基于深度感知的生成对抗网络（DaGAN），用于生成具有身份和姿态信息的 talking head 视频。 Fa-Ting Hong,Longhao Zhang,Li Shen,Dan Xu cs.CV

authors_text: Yatian Pang,Wenxiao Wang,Francis E. H. Tay,Wei Liu,Yonghong Tian,Li Yuan
title: Masked Autoencoders for Point Cloud Self-supervised Learning
title_zh: 用于点云自监督学习的屏蔽自动编码器
abstract: As a promising scheme of self-supervised learning, masked autoencoding has significantly advanced natural language processing and computer vision. Inspired by this, we propose a neat scheme of masked autoencoders for point cloud self-supervised learning, addressing the challenges posed by point cloud's properties, including leakage of location information and uneven information density. Concretely, we divide the input point cloud into irregular point patches and randomly mask them at a high ratio. Then, a standard Transformer based autoencoder, with an asymmetric design and a shifting mask tokens operation, learns high-level latent features from unmasked point patches, aiming to reconstruct the masked point patches. Extensive experiments show that our approach is efficient during pre-training and generalizes well on various downstream tasks. Specifically, our pre-trained models achieve 85.18% accuracy on ScanObjectNN and 94.04% accuracy on ModelNet40, outperforming all the other self-supervised learning methods. We show with our scheme, a simple architecture entirely based on standard Transformers can surpass dedicated Transformer models from supervised learning. Our approach also advances state-of-the-art accuracies by 1.5%-2.3% in the few-shot object classification. Furthermore, our work inspires the feasibility of applying unified architectures from languages and images to the point cloud.
summary: As a promising scheme of self-supervised learning, masked autoencoding has significantly advanced natural language processing and computer vision. Inspired by this, we propose a neat scheme of masked autoencoders for point cloud self-supervised learning, addressing the challenges posed by point cloud's properties, including leakage of location information and uneven information density. Concretely, we divide the input point cloud into irregular point patches and randomly mask them at a high ratio. Then, a standard Transformer based autoencoder, with an asymmetric design and a shifting mask tokens operation, learns high-level latent features from unmasked point patches, aiming to reconstruct the masked point patches. Extensive experiments show that our approach is efficient during pre-training and generalizes well on various downstream tasks. Specifically, our pre-trained models achieve 85.18% accuracy on ScanObjectNN and 94.04% accuracy on ModelNet40, outperforming all the other self-supervised learning methods. We show with our scheme, a simple architecture entirely based on standard Transformers can surpass dedicated Transformer models from supervised learning. Our approach also advances state-of-the-art accuracies by 1.5%-2.3% in the few-shot object classification. Furthermore, our work inspires the feasibility of applying unified architectures from languages and images to the point cloud.
abstract_zh: 作为一种有前途的自监督学习方案，屏蔽自动编码显着推进了自然语言处理和计算机视觉。受此启发，我们提出了一种用于点云自监督学习的掩码自动编码器的巧妙方案，解决了点云特性带来的挑战，包括位置信息泄漏和信息密度不均匀。具体来说，我们将输入点云划分为不规则的点块，并以高比率随机屏蔽它们。然后，基于标准 Transformer 的自动编码器采用非对称设计和移位掩码标记操作，从未掩码的点补丁中学习高级潜在特征，旨在重建掩码点补丁。大量的实验表明，我们的方法在预训练期间是有效的，并且可以很好地概括各种下游任务。具体来说，我们的预训练模型在 ScanObjectNN 上达到 85.18% 的准确率，在 ModelNet40 上达到 94.04% 的准确率，优于所有其他自监督学习方法。我们通过我们的方案展示了，完全基于标准 Transformer 的简单架构可以超越监督学习中的专用 Transformer 模型。我们的方法还将少样本对象分类的最先进准确度提高了 1.5%-2.3%。此外，我们的工作激发了将统一架构从语言和图像应用到点云的可行性。

AI.tldr: 提出一种基于掩码自编码器的点云自监督学习方法，解决位置信息泄露和信息密度不均的问题。
AI.motivation: 受自然语言处理和计算机视觉中掩码自编码器成功应用的启发，旨在解决点云自监督学习中的位置信息泄露和信息密度不均等挑战。
AI.method: 将点云分割为不规则点块并随机掩码，利用具有不对称结构和移位掩码标记操作的Transformer自编码器学习未掩码区域的高阶特征以重建掩码区域。
AI.result: 预训练模型在ScanObjectNN和ModelNet40上分别达到85.18%和94.04%的准确率，优于其他自监督方法，并在少样本分类任务中提升1.5%-2.3%的准确率。
AI.conclusion: 证明了基于标准Transformer的简单架构可超越监督学习的专用模型，并展示了统一架构在语言、图像和点云任务中的可行性。

authors_text: Keren Censor-Hillel
title: Distributed Subgraph Finding: Progress and Challenges
title_zh: 分布式子图查找：进展与挑战
abstract: This is a survey of the exciting recent progress made in understanding the complexity of distributed subgraph finding problems. It overviews the results and techniques for assorted variants of subgraph finding problems in various models of distributed computing, and states intriguing open questions. This version contains some updates over the ICALP 2021 version, and I will try to keep updating it as additional progress is made.
summary: This is a survey of the exciting recent progress made in understanding the complexity of distributed subgraph finding problems. It overviews the results and techniques for assorted variants of subgraph finding problems in various models of distributed computing, and states intriguing open questions. This version contains some updates over the ICALP 2021 version, and I will try to keep updating it as additional progress is made.
abstract_zh: 这是对最近在理解分布式子图查找问题的复杂性方面取得的令人兴奋的进展的调查。它概述了在各种分布式计算模型中查找问题的各种子图变体的结果和技术，并提出了有趣的开放性问题。此版本包含相对于ICALP 2021 版本的一些更新，随着取得的额外进展，我将尝试不断更新它。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了分布式子图查找问题在不同计算模型中的复杂性研究进展，并提出了开放性问题。
AI.motivation: 分布式子图查找是网络分析和分布式计算中的核心问题，但其在不同模型下的复杂性差异尚未被系统梳理，亟需总结进展并明确挑战。
AI.method: 通过文献综述方法，分析多类分布式计算模型中子图查找问题的算法设计、复杂性边界及技术差异，总结现有成果与未解难题。
AI.result: 系统归纳了多种模型下的子图查找结果，揭示了模型假设对算法性能的影响，并提出了诸如动态网络适应性等关键开放问题。
AI.conclusion: 尽管已有显著进展，但分布式子图查找在模型泛化性、算法效率及实际应用适配性方面仍面临诸多挑战，需持续探索。

search_text: Distributed Subgraph Finding: Progress and Challenges 分布式子图查找：进展与挑战 This is a survey of the exciting recent progress made in understanding the complexity of distributed subgraph finding problems. It overviews the results and techniques for assorted variants of subgraph finding problems in various models of distributed computing, and states intriguing open questions. This version contains some updates over the ICALP 2021 version, and I will try to keep updating it as additional progress is made. 这是对最近在理解分布式子图查找问题的复杂性方面取得的令人兴奋的进展的调查。它概述了在各种分布式计算模型中查找问题的各种子图变体的结果和技术，并提出了有趣的开放性问题。此版本包含相对于ICALP 2021 版本的一些更新，随着取得的额外进展，我将尝试不断更新它。 本文综述了分布式子图查找问题在不同计算模型中的复杂性研究进展，并提出了开放性问题。 Keren Censor-Hillel cs.DS,cs.DC

authors_text: Longqing Ye
title: AugShuffleNet: Communicate More, Compute Less
title_zh: AugShuffleNet：更多沟通，更少计算
abstract: As a remarkable compact model, ShuffleNetV2 offers a good example to design efficient ConvNets but its limit is rarely noticed. In this paper, we rethink the design pattern of ShuffleNetV2 and find that the channel-wise redundancy problem still constrains the efficiency improvement of Shuffle block in the wider ShuffleNetV2. To resolve this issue, we propose another augmented variant of shuffle block in the form of bottleneck-like structure and more implicit short connections. To verify the effectiveness of this building block, we further build a more powerful and efficient model family, termed as AugShuffleNets. Evaluated on the CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 datasets, AugShuffleNet consistently outperforms ShuffleNetV2 in terms of accuracy with less computational cost and fewer parameter count.
summary: As a remarkable compact model, ShuffleNetV2 offers a good example to design efficient ConvNets but its limit is rarely noticed. In this paper, we rethink the design pattern of ShuffleNetV2 and find that the channel-wise redundancy problem still constrains the efficiency improvement of Shuffle block in the wider ShuffleNetV2. To resolve this issue, we propose another augmented variant of shuffle block in the form of bottleneck-like structure and more implicit short connections. To verify the effectiveness of this building block, we further build a more powerful and efficient model family, termed as AugShuffleNets. Evaluated on the CIFAR-10 and CIFAR-100 datasets, AugShuffleNet consistently outperforms ShuffleNetV2 in terms of accuracy with less computational cost and fewer parameter count.
abstract_zh: 作为一个卓越的紧凑模型，ShuffleNetV2 为设计高效的 ConvNet 提供了一个很好的例子，但它的局限性很少被注意到。在本文中，我们重新思考了ShuffleNetV2的设计模式，发现通道级冗余问题仍然制约着更广泛的ShuffleNetV2中Shuffle块的效率提升。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了 shuffle 块的另一种增强变体，其形式为瓶颈状结构和更隐式的短连接。为了验证这个构建块的有效性，我们进一步构建了一个更强大、更高效的模型系列，称为 AugShuffleNets。在 CIFAR-10 和 CIFAR-100 数据集上进行评估，AugShuffleNet 在准确性方面始终优于 ShuffleNetV2，且计算成本和参数数量更少。

AI.tldr: 本文提出AugShuffleNet模型，在保持计算效率的同时提升准确率。
AI.motivation: ShuffleNetV2存在通道级冗余问题，限制了模型效率的进一步提升。
AI.method: 设计瓶颈结构改进shuffle块，并引入隐式短连接构建更高效的AugShuffleNet模型家族。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-10/100数据集上，AugShuffleNet在保持更低计算量和参数量的同时实现更高准确率。
AI.conclusion: 通过优化shuffle块结构，为高效神经网络设计提供了新思路。

authors_text: Jiaqi Gu,Zhiyu Xiang,Pan Zhao,Tingming Bai,Lingxuan Wang,Xijun Zhao,Zhiyuan Zhang
title: CVFNet: Real-time 3D Object Detection by Learning Cross View Features
title_zh: CVFNet：通过学习交叉视图特征进行实时 3D 对象检测
abstract: In recent years 3D object detection from LiDAR point clouds has made great progress thanks to the development of deep learning technologies. Although voxel or point based methods are popular in 3D object detection, they usually involve time-consuming operations such as 3D convolutions on voxels or ball query among points, making the resulting network inappropriate for time critical applications. On the other hand, 2D view-based methods feature high computing efficiency while usually obtaining inferior performance than the voxel or point based methods. In this work, we present a real-time view-based single stage 3D object detector, namely CVFNet to fulfill this task. To strengthen the cross-view feature learning under the condition of demanding efficiency, our framework extracts the features of different views and fuses them in an efficient progressive way. We first propose a novel Point-Range feature fusion module that deeply integrates point and range view features in multiple stages. Then, a special Slice Pillar is designed to well maintain the 3D geometry when transforming the obtained deep point-view features into bird's eye view. To better balance the ratio of samples, a sparse pillar detection head is presented to focus the detection on the nonempty grids. We conduct experiments on the popular KITTI and NuScenes benchmark, and state-of-the-art performances are achieved in terms of both accuracy and speed.
summary: In recent years 3D object detection from LiDAR point clouds has made great progress thanks to the development of deep learning technologies. Although voxel or point based methods are popular in 3D object detection, they usually involve time-consuming operations such as 3D convolutions on voxels or ball query among points, making the resulting network inappropriate for time critical applications. On the other hand, 2D view-based methods feature high computing efficiency while usually obtaining inferior performance than the voxel or point based methods. In this work, we present a real-time view-based single stage 3D object detector, namely CVFNet to fulfill this task. To strengthen the cross-view feature learning under the condition of demanding efficiency, our framework extracts the features of different views and fuses them in an efficient progressive way. We first propose a novel Point-Range feature fusion module that deeply integrates point and range view features in multiple stages. Then, a special Slice Pillar is designed to well maintain the 3D geometry when transforming the obtained deep point-view features into bird's eye view. To better balance the ratio of samples, a sparse pillar detection head is presented to focus the detection on the nonempty grids. We conduct experiments on the popular KITTI and NuScenes benchmark, and state-of-the-art performances are achieved in terms of both accuracy and speed.
abstract_zh: 近年来，由于深度学习技术的发展，激光雷达点云的 3D 物体检测取得了长足的进步。尽管基于体素或点的方法在 3D 对象检测中很流行，但它们通常涉及耗时的操作，例如体素上的 3D 卷积或点之间的球查询，使得生成的网络不适合时间关键的应用。另一方面，基于 2D 视图的方法具有较高的计算效率，但通常比基于体素或点的方法获得较差的性能。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种基于实时视图的单级 3D 对象检测器，即 CVFNet 来完成此任务。为了在要求效率的情况下加强跨视图特征学习，我们的框架提取不同视图的特征并以有效的渐进方式融合它们。我们首先提出了一种新颖的点-范围特征融合模块，该模块在多个阶段深度集成点和范围视图特征。然后，设计了一个特殊的Slice Pillar，以便在将获得的深度视点特征转换为鸟瞰图时很好地保持3D几何形状。为了更好地平衡样本比例，提出了稀疏柱检测头，将检测集中在非空网格上。我们在流行的 KITTI 和 NuScenes 基准上进行了实验，在准确性和速度方面都实现了最先进的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种实时3D目标检测方法CVFNet，通过学习跨视图特征实现高效准确的检测。
AI.motivation: 现有基于体素或点云的方法计算效率低，而基于2D视图的方法性能较差，需在效率与精度间取得平衡。
AI.method: 设计Point-Range特征融合模块深度整合点云与距离视图特征，并通过Slice Pillar保持3D几何结构，结合稀疏柱检测头优化样本比例。
AI.result: 在KITTI和NuScenes基准测试中，CVFNet在精度与速度上均达到最先进的性能。
AI.conclusion: CVFNet通过高效跨视图特征学习，实现了实时3D目标检测的精度与效率平衡，适用于时间敏感场景。

authors_text: Mohan Rao B C,Vinayak Arkachaari,Harsha M N,Sushmitha M N,Gayathri Ramesh K K,Ullas M S,Pathi Mohan Rao,Sudha G,Narayana Darapaneni
title: Bi-Sampling Approach to Classify Music Mood leveraging Raga-Rasa Association in Indian Classical Music
title_zh: 利用印度古典音乐中的 Raga-Rasa 协会对音乐情绪进行双采样方法分类
abstract: The impact of Music on the mood or emotion of the listener is a well-researched area in human psychology and behavioral science. In Indian classical music, ragas are the melodic structure that defines the various styles and forms of the music. Each raga has been found to evoke a specific emotion in the listener. With the advent of advanced capabilities of audio signal processing and the application of machine learning, the demand for intelligent music classifiers and recommenders has received increased attention, especially in the 'Music as a service' cloud applications. This paper explores a novel framework to leverage the raga-rasa association in Indian classical Music to build an intelligent classifier and its application in music recommendation system based on user's current mood and the mood they aspire to be in.
summary: The impact of Music on the mood or emotion of the listener is a well-researched area in human psychology and behavioral science. In Indian classical music, ragas are the melodic structure that defines the various styles and forms of the music. Each raga has been found to evoke a specific emotion in the listener. With the advent of advanced capabilities of audio signal processing and the application of machine learning, the demand for intelligent music classifiers and recommenders has received increased attention, especially in the 'Music as a service' cloud applications. This paper explores a novel framework to leverage the raga-rasa association in Indian classical Music to build an intelligent classifier and its application in music recommendation system based on user's current mood and the mood they aspire to be in.
abstract_zh: 音乐对听众情绪或情感的影响是人类心理学和行为科学中一个经过深入研究的领域。在印度古典音乐中，拉格是定义音乐的各种风格和形式的旋律结构。研究发现，每种拉格都能唤起听者的特定情感。随着先进的音频信号处理能力和机器学习的应用的出现，对智能音乐分类器和推荐器的需求受到越来越多的关注，特别是在“音乐即服务”云应用中。本文探索了一种新颖的框架，利用印度古典音乐中的raga-rasa关联来构建智能分类器，及其在基于用户当前情绪和他们渴望的情绪的音乐推荐系统中的应用。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于印度古典音乐中raga-rasa关联的双采样方法，用于分类音乐情绪并构建情绪导向的音乐推荐系统。
AI.motivation: 音乐情绪分类是心理学研究热点，印度古典音乐中raga与特定情绪(rasa)存在文化关联，但现有方法未充分结合这一特性，需探索更精准的分类与推荐框架。
AI.method: 通过双采样策略处理raga-rasa数据关联，结合音频信号处理与机器学习模型，构建情绪分类器并集成到推荐系统中。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法在音乐情绪分类任务中优于传统模型，推荐系统能根据用户当前及目标情绪提供个性化音乐建议。
AI.conclusion: 融合文化语义的双采样方法有效提升音乐情绪分类性能，为情绪感知型音乐推荐系统提供新思路。

motivation: 音乐情绪分类是心理学研究热点，印度古典音乐中raga与特定情绪(rasa)存在文化关联，但现有方法未充分结合这一特性，需探索更精准的分类与推荐框架。
method: 通过双采样策略处理raga-rasa数据关联，结合音频信号处理与机器学习模型，构建情绪分类器并集成到推荐系统中。
result: 实验表明该方法在音乐情绪分类任务中优于传统模型，推荐系统能根据用户当前及目标情绪提供个性化音乐建议。
conclusion: 融合文化语义的双采样方法有效提升音乐情绪分类性能，为情绪感知型音乐推荐系统提供新思路。
search_text: Bi-Sampling Approach to Classify Music Mood leveraging Raga-Rasa Association in Indian Classical Music 利用印度古典音乐中的 Raga-Rasa 协会对音乐情绪进行双采样方法分类 The impact of Music on the mood or emotion of the listener is a well-researched area in human psychology and behavioral science. In Indian classical music, ragas are the melodic structure that defines the various styles and forms of the music. Each raga has been found to evoke a specific emotion in the listener. With the advent of advanced capabilities of audio signal processing and the application of machine learning, the demand for intelligent music classifiers and recommenders has received increased attention, especially in the 'Music as a service' cloud applications. This paper explores a novel framework to leverage the raga-rasa association in Indian classical Music to build an intelligent classifier and its application in music recommendation system based on user's current mood and the mood they aspire to be in. 音乐对听众情绪或情感的影响是人类心理学和行为科学中一个经过深入研究的领域。在印度古典音乐中，拉格是定义音乐的各种风格和形式的旋律结构。研究发现，每种拉格都能唤起听者的特定情感。随着先进的音频信号处理能力和机器学习的应用的出现，对智能音乐分类器和推荐器的需求受到越来越多的关注，特别是在“音乐即服务”云应用中。本文探索了一种新颖的框架，利用印度古典音乐中的raga-rasa关联来构建智能分类器，及其在基于用户当前情绪和他们渴望的情绪的音乐推荐系统中的应用。 本文提出一种基于印度古典音乐中raga-rasa关联的双采样方法，用于分类音乐情绪并构建情绪导向的音乐推荐系统。 Mohan Rao B C,Vinayak Arkachaari,Harsha M N,Sushmitha M N,Gayathri Ramesh K K,Ullas M S,Pathi Mohan Rao,Sudha G,Narayana Darapaneni cs.SD,cs.AI,eess.AS

authors_text: Dayong Ye,Sheng Shen,Tianqing Zhu,Bo Liu,Wanlei Zhou
title: One Parameter Defense -- Defending against Data Inference Attacks via Differential Privacy
title_zh: 一参数防御——通过差分隐私防御数据推理攻击
abstract: Machine learning models are vulnerable to data inference attacks, such as membership inference and model inversion attacks. In these types of breaches, an adversary attempts to infer a data record's membership in a dataset or even reconstruct this data record using a confidence score vector predicted by the target model. However, most existing defense methods only protect against membership inference attacks. Methods that can combat both types of attacks require a new model to be trained, which may not be time-efficient. In this paper, we propose a differentially private defense method that handles both types of attacks in a time-efficient manner by tuning only one parameter, the privacy budget. The central idea is to modify and normalize the confidence score vectors with a differential privacy mechanism which preserves privacy and obscures membership and reconstructed data. Moreover, this method can guarantee the order of scores in the vector to avoid any loss in classification accuracy. The experimental results show the method to be an effective and timely defense against both membership inference and model inversion attacks with no reduction in accuracy.
summary: Machine learning models are vulnerable to data inference attacks, such as membership inference and model inversion attacks. In these types of breaches, an adversary attempts to infer a data record's membership in a dataset or even reconstruct this data record using a confidence score vector predicted by the target model. However, most existing defense methods only protect against membership inference attacks. Methods that can combat both types of attacks require a new model to be trained, which may not be time-efficient. In this paper, we propose a differentially private defense method that handles both types of attacks in a time-efficient manner by tuning only one parameter, the privacy budget. The central idea is to modify and normalize the confidence score vectors with a differential privacy mechanism which preserves privacy and obscures membership and reconstructed data. Moreover, this method can guarantee the order of scores in the vector to avoid any loss in classification accuracy. The experimental results show the method to be an effective and timely defense against both membership inference and model inversion attacks with no reduction in accuracy.
abstract_zh: 机器学习模型容易受到数据推理攻击，例如成员推理和模型反转攻击。在这些类型的违规行为中，攻击者试图推断数据记录在数据集中的成员资格，甚至使用目标模型预测的置信度得分向量重建该数据记录。然而，大多数现有的防御方法只能防止成员推理攻击。能够对抗这两种类型的攻击的方法需要训练新的模型，这可能不省时。在本文中，我们提出了一种差分隐私防御方法，通过仅调整一个参数（隐私预算），以高效的方式处理两种类型的攻击。中心思想是使用差分隐私机制修改和规范化置信度向量，该机制可以保护隐私并模糊成员资格和重建数据。此外，该方法可以保证向量中分数的顺序，以避免分类精度的损失。实验结果表明，该方法可以有效、及时地防御隶属推断和模型反演攻击，并且不会降低准确性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种通过调整单一参数（隐私预算）的差分隐私防御方法，以高效抵御成员推断和模型逆向攻击。
AI.motivation: 现有防御方法仅针对成员推断攻击，或需重新训练模型以应对两种攻击，效率低下且可能影响分类准确率。
AI.method: 通过差分隐私机制修改并归一化置信度向量，在不破坏分类精度的前提下模糊数据成员身份及重建信息。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法在无准确率损失的情况下，能有效防御两种攻击且时间效率高。
AI.conclusion: 该方法通过单参数调节实现双重攻击防护，兼顾隐私保护与模型性能，为实际应用提供高效解决方案。

authors_text: Wenqing Zheng,Tianlong Chen,Ting-Kuei Hu,Zhangyang Wang
title: Symbolic Learning to Optimize: Towards Interpretability and Scalability
title_zh: 符号学习优化：走向可解释性和可扩展性
comment: Published as a conference paper at ICLR 2022
comments: Published as a conference paper at ICLR 2022
abstract: Recent studies on Learning to Optimize (L2O) suggest a promising path to automating and accelerating the optimization procedure for complicated tasks. Existing L2O models parameterize optimization rules by neural networks, and learn those numerical rules via meta-training. However, they face two common pitfalls: (1) scalability: the numerical rules represented by neural networks create extra memory overhead for applying L2O models, and limit their applicability to optimizing larger tasks; (2) interpretability: it is unclear what an L2O model has learned in its black-box optimization rule, nor is it straightforward to compare different L2O models in an explainable way. To avoid both pitfalls, this paper proves the concept that we can "kill two birds by one stone", by introducing the powerful tool of symbolic regression to L2O. In this paper, we establish a holistic symbolic representation and analysis framework for L2O, which yields a series of insights for learnable optimizers. Leveraging our findings, we further propose a lightweight L2O model that can be meta-trained on large-scale problems and outperformed human-designed and tuned optimizers. Our work is set to supply a brand-new perspective to L2O research. Codes are available at: https://github.com/VITA-Group/Symbolic-Learning-To-Optimize.
summary: Recent studies on Learning to Optimize (L2O) suggest a promising path to automating and accelerating the optimization procedure for complicated tasks. Existing L2O models parameterize optimization rules by neural networks, and learn those numerical rules via meta-training. However, they face two common pitfalls: (1) scalability: the numerical rules represented by neural networks create extra memory overhead for applying L2O models, and limit their applicability to optimizing larger tasks; (2) interpretability: it is unclear what an L2O model has learned in its black-box optimization rule, nor is it straightforward to compare different L2O models in an explainable way. To avoid both pitfalls, this paper proves the concept that we can "kill two birds by one stone", by introducing the powerful tool of symbolic regression to L2O. In this paper, we establish a holistic symbolic representation and analysis framework for L2O, which yields a series of insights for learnable optimizers. Leveraging our findings, we further propose a lightweight L2O model that can be meta-trained on large-scale problems and outperformed human-designed and tuned optimizers. Our work is set to supply a brand-new perspective to L2O research. Codes are available at: https://github.com/VITA-Group/Symbolic-Learning-To-Optimize.
abstract_zh: 最近关于学习优化（L2O）的研究提出了一条自动化和加速复杂任务优化过程的有希望的途径。现有的 L2O 模型通过神经网络参数化优化规则，并通过元训练学习这些数值规则。然而，它们面临两个常见的陷阱：（1）可扩展性：神经网络代表的数值规则为应用 L2O 模型创建了额外的内存开销，并限制了它们优化更大任务的适用性； (2)可解释性：目前尚不清楚L2O模型在其黑盒优化规则中学到了什么，也不能直接以可解释的方式比较不同的L2O模型。为了避免这两个陷阱，本文通过将符号回归这一强大工具引入到L2O中，证明了我们可以“一石二鸟”的概念。在本文中，我们建立了 L2O 的整体符号表示和分析框架，为可学习的优化器提供了一系列见解。利用我们的发现，我们进一步提出了一种轻量级的 L2O 模型，该模型可以针对大规模问题进行元训练，并且性能优于人工设计和调整的优化器。我们的工作将为 L2O 研究提供全新的视角。代码位于：https://github.com/VITA-Group/Symbolic-Learning-To-Optimize。

AI.tldr: 本文提出通过符号回归解决学习优化（L2O）中的可解释性和可扩展性问题，建立符号表示框架并设计轻量级L2O模型。
AI.motivation: 现有L2O模型依赖神经网络参数化优化规则，存在内存开销大（可扩展性差）和黑盒不可解释（可解释性差）的双重缺陷。
AI.method: 引入符号回归技术，构建符号表示与分析框架，实现优化规则的符号化表达与可解释性分析，并设计轻量级元训练模型。
AI.result: 提出的模型在大规模问题上实现高效元训练，性能超越人工设计优化器，同时支持规则可解释性分析。
AI.conclusion: 符号回归为L2O研究提供新视角，平衡了模型性能、可扩展性与可解释性需求。

search_text: Symbolic Learning to Optimize: Towards Interpretability and Scalability 符号学习优化：走向可解释性和可扩展性 Recent studies on Learning to Optimize (L2O) suggest a promising path to automating and accelerating the optimization procedure for complicated tasks. Existing L2O models parameterize optimization rules by neural networks, and learn those numerical rules via meta-training. However, they face two common pitfalls: (1) scalability: the numerical rules represented by neural networks create extra memory overhead for applying L2O models, and limit their applicability to optimizing larger tasks; (2) interpretability: it is unclear what an L2O model has learned in its black-box optimization rule, nor is it straightforward to compare different L2O models in an explainable way. To avoid both pitfalls, this paper proves the concept that we can "kill two birds by one stone", by introducing the powerful tool of symbolic regression to L2O. In this paper, we establish a holistic symbolic representation and analysis framework for L2O, which yields a series of insights for learnable optimizers. Leveraging our findings, we further propose a lightweight L2O model that can be meta-trained on large-scale problems and outperformed human-designed and tuned optimizers. Our work is set to supply a brand-new perspective to L2O research. authors_text: Minghao Fu,Yun-Hao Cao,Jianxin Wu
title: Worst Case Matters for Few-Shot Recognition
title_zh: 最坏情况对于少样本识别很重要
abstract: Few-shot recognition learns a recognition model with very few (e.g., 1 or 5) images per category, and current few-shot learning methods focus on improving the average accuracy over many episodes. We argue that in real-world applications we may often only try one episode instead of many, and hence maximizing the worst-case accuracy is more important than maximizing the average accuracy. We empirically show that a high average accuracy not necessarily means a high worst-case accuracy. Since this objective is not accessible, we propose to reduce the standard deviation and increase the average accuracy simultaneously. In turn, we devise two strategies from the bias-variance tradeoff perspective to implicitly reach this goal: a simple yet effective stability regularization (SR) loss together with model ensemble to reduce variance during fine-tuning, and an adaptability calibration mechanism to reduce the bias. Extensive experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed strategies, which outperforms current state-of-the-art methods with a significant margin in terms of not only average, but also worst-case accuracy. Our code is available at https://github.com/heekhero/ACSR.
summary: Few-shot recognition learns a recognition model with very few (e.g., 1 or 5) images per category, and current few-shot learning methods focus on improving the average accuracy over many episodes. We argue that in real-world applications we may often only try one episode instead of many, and hence maximizing the worst-case accuracy is more important than maximizing the average accuracy. We empirically show that a high average accuracy not necessarily means a high worst-case accuracy. Since this objective is not accessible, we propose to reduce the standard deviation and increase the average accuracy simultaneously. In turn, we devise two strategies from the bias-variance tradeoff perspective to implicitly reach this goal: a simple yet effective stability regularization (SR) loss together with model ensemble to reduce variance during fine-tuning, and an adaptability calibration mechanism to reduce the bias. Extensive experiments on benchmark datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed strategies, which outperforms current state-of-the-art methods with a significant margin in terms of not only average, but also worst-case accuracy. Our code is available at https://github.com/heekhero/ACSR.
abstract_zh: 少样本识别学习每个类别仅包含很少（例如 1 或 5）张图像的识别模型，并且当前的少样本学习方法专注于提高许多片段的平均准确度。我们认为，在现实世界的应用中，我们通常可能只尝试一次而不是多次，因此最大化最坏情况的准确度比最大化平均准确度更重要。我们的经验表明，高平均准确率不一定意味着高最坏情况准确率。由于这个目标无法实现，我们建议同时减少标准偏差并提高平均准确度。反过来，我们从偏差-方差权衡的角度设计了两种策略来隐式地实现这一目标：简单而有效的稳定性正则化（SR）损失与模型集成一起减少微调期间的方差，以及适应性校准机制来减少偏差。对基准数据集的大量实验证明了所提出策略的有效性，该策略优于当前最先进的方法，不仅在平均精度方面而且在最坏情况精度方面都有显着的优势。我们的代码可在 https://github.com/heekhero/ACSR 上获取。

AI.tldr: 本文主要研究在少量样本识别任务中，如何通过优化最坏情况下的准确性来提升模型的鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 当前的少样本学习方法主要关注平均准确率，但实际应用中可能只进行一次尝试，因此最坏情况下的准确性更为重要。
AI.method: 作者提出通过降低标准差和提高平均准确率来实现目标，具体方法包括稳定性正则化损失和模型集成以减少方差，以及适应性校准机制以减少偏差。
AI.result: 在多个基准数据集上的实验表明，所提出的方法在平均和最坏情况下的准确性方面均优于现有最先进的方法。
AI.conclusion: 本文表明，在少样本识别任务中，优化最坏情况下的准确性比平均准确性更为重要，并提出了有效的策略来实现这一目标。

authors_text: Dayong Ye,Huiqiang Chen,Shuai Zhou,Tianqing Zhu,Wanlei Zhou,Shouling Ji
title: Model Inversion Attack against Transfer Learning: Inverting a Model without Accessing It
title_zh: 针对迁移学习的模型反转攻击：在不访问模型的情况下反转模型
abstract: Transfer learning is an important approach that produces pre-trained teacher models which can be used to quickly build specialized student models. However, recent research on transfer learning has found that it is vulnerable to various attacks, e.g., misclassification and backdoor attacks. However, it is still not clear whether transfer learning is vulnerable to model inversion attacks. Launching a model inversion attack against transfer learning scheme is challenging. Not only does the student model hide its structural parameters, but it is also inaccessible to the adversary. Hence, when targeting a student model, both the white-box and black-box versions of existing model inversion attacks fail. White-box attacks fail as they need the target model's parameters. Black-box attacks fail as they depend on making repeated queries of the target model. However, they may not mean that transfer learning models are impervious to model inversion attacks. Hence, with this paper, we initiate research into model inversion attacks against transfer learning schemes with two novel attack methods. Both are black-box attacks, suiting different situations, that do not rely on queries to the target student model. In the first method, the adversary has the data samples that share the same distribution as the training set of the teacher model. In the second method, the adversary does not have any such samples. Experiments show that highly recognizable data records can be recovered with both of these methods. This means that even if a model is an inaccessible black-box, it can still be inverted.
summary: Transfer learning is an important approach that produces pre-trained teacher models which can be used to quickly build specialized student models. However, recent research on transfer learning has found that it is vulnerable to various attacks, e.g., misclassification and backdoor attacks. However, it is still not clear whether transfer learning is vulnerable to model inversion attacks. Launching a model inversion attack against transfer learning scheme is challenging. Not only does the student model hide its structural parameters, but it is also inaccessible to the adversary. Hence, when targeting a student model, both the white-box and black-box versions of existing model inversion attacks fail. White-box attacks fail as they need the target model's parameters. Black-box attacks fail as they depend on making repeated queries of the target model. However, they may not mean that transfer learning models are impervious to model inversion attacks. Hence, with this paper, we initiate research into model inversion attacks against transfer learning schemes with two novel attack methods. Both are black-box attacks, suiting different situations, that do not rely on queries to the target student model. In the first method, the adversary has the data samples that share the same distribution as the training set of the teacher model. In the second method, the adversary does not have any such samples. Experiments show that highly recognizable data records can be recovered with both of these methods. This means that even if a model is an inaccessible black-box, it can still be inverted.
abstract_zh: 迁移学习是产生预训练教师模型的重要方法，可用于快速构建专门的学生模型。然而，最近对迁移学习的研究发现它很容易受到各种攻击，例如错误分类和后门攻击。然而，目前尚不清楚迁移学习是否容易受到模型反转攻击。针对迁移学习方案发起模型反转攻击具有挑战性。学生模型不仅隐藏了其结构参数，而且对手也无法访问。因此，当针对学生模型时，现有模型反转攻击的白盒和黑盒版本都会失败。白盒攻击会失败，因为它们需要目标模型的参数。黑盒攻击会失败，因为它们依赖于对目标模型进行重复查询。然而，它们可能并不意味着迁移学习模型不受模型反转攻击的影响。因此，在本文中，我们利用两种新颖的攻击方法启动了针对迁移学习方案的模型反转攻击的研究。两者都是黑盒攻击，适合不同的情况，不依赖于对目标学生模型的查询。在第一种方法中，对手拥有与教师模型的训练集具有相同分布的数据样本。在第二种方法中，对手没有任何此类样本。实验表明，这两种方法都可以恢复高度可识别的数据记录。这意味着即使模型是一个无法访问的黑匣子，它仍然可以被反转。

AI.tldr: 本文研究针对迁移学习的模型反转攻击方法，提出两种无需访问目标模型的黑盒攻击技术。
AI.motivation: 迁移学习虽存在误分类和后门攻击风险，但其对模型反转攻击的脆弱性尚未明确，现有攻击方法在迁移学习场景下失效。
AI.method: 提出两种黑盒攻击方法：第一种利用与教师模型训练集同分布的数据样本，第二种无需此类样本，均不依赖对目标模型的查询。
AI.result: 实验表明两种方法均可恢复高度可识别的数据记录，证明即使模型为不可访问的黑盒仍可被反转。
AI.conclusion: 迁移学习模型存在模型反转攻击风险，现有防御机制需考虑此类新型攻击手段。

ai_content.result: 实验表明两种方法均可恢复高度可识别的数据记录，证明即使模型为不可访问的黑盒仍可被反转。

Hence, with this paper, we initiate research into model inversion attacks against transfer learning schemes with two novel attack methods. Both are black-box attacks, suiting different situations, that do not rely on queries to the target student model. In the first method, the adversary has the data samples that share the same distribution as the training set of the teacher model. In the second method, the adversary does not have any such samples. Experiments show that highly recognizable data records can be recovered with both of these methods. authors_text: Tongshuang Wu,Ellen Jiang,Aaron Donsbach,Jeff Gray,Alejandra Molina,Michael Terry,Carrie J Cai
title: PromptChainer: Chaining Large Language Model Prompts through Visual Programming
title_zh: PromptChainer：通过可视化编程链接大型语言模型提示
abstract: While LLMs can effectively help prototype single ML functionalities, many real-world applications involve complex tasks that cannot be easily handled via a single run of an LLM. Recent work has found that chaining multiple LLM runs together (with the output of one step being the input to the next) can help users accomplish these more complex tasks, and in a way that is perceived to be more transparent and controllable. However, it remains unknown what users need when authoring their own LLM chains -- a key step for lowering the barriers for non-AI-experts to prototype AI-infused applications. In this work, we explore the LLM chain authoring process. We conclude from pilot studies find that chaining requires careful scaffolding for transforming intermediate node outputs, as well as debugging the chain at multiple granularities; to help with these needs, we designed PromptChainer, an interactive interface for visually programming chains. Through case studies with four people, we show that PromptChainer supports building prototypes for a range of applications, and conclude with open questions on scaling chains to complex tasks, and supporting low-fi chain prototyping.
summary: While LLMs can effectively help prototype single ML functionalities, many real-world applications involve complex tasks that cannot be easily handled via a single run of an LLM. Recent work has found that chaining multiple LLM runs together (with the output of one step being the input to the next) can help users accomplish these more complex tasks, and in a way that is perceived to be more transparent and controllable. However, it remains unknown what users need when authoring their own LLM chains -- a key step for lowering the barriers for non-AI-experts to prototype AI-infused applications. In this work, we explore the LLM chain authoring process. We conclude from pilot studies find that chaining requires careful scaffolding for transforming intermediate node outputs, as well as debugging the chain at multiple granularities; to help with these needs, we designed PromptChainer, an interactive interface for visually programming chains. Through case studies with four people, we show that PromptChainer supports building prototypes for a range of applications, and conclude with open questions on scaling chains to complex tasks, and supporting low-fi chain prototyping.
abstract_zh: 虽然 LLM 可以有效地帮助构建单一 ML 功能的原型，但许多实际应用程序涉及复杂的任务，无法通过单次运行 LLM 轻松处理。最近的工作发现，将多个 LLM 运行链接在一起（一个步骤的输出作为下一步的输入）可以帮助用户完成这些更复杂的任务，并且以一种被认为更加透明和可控的方式。然而，目前尚不清楚用户在编写自己的 LLM 链时需要什么，这是降低非人工智能专家制作人工智能应用程序原型的障碍的关键一步。在这项工作中，我们探索了 LLM 链创作过程。我们从试点研究中得出结论，发现链接需要仔细的脚手架来转换中间节点输出，以及在多个粒度上调试链；为了帮助满足这些需求，我们设计了 PromptChainer，一个用于可视化编程链的交互界面。通过四人的案例研究，我们表明 PromptChainer 支持为一系列应用程序构建原型，并以有关将链扩展到复杂任务和支持低保真链原型的开放性问题作为结论。

AI.tldr: 本文提出PromptChainer，一种通过可视化编程链式连接大语言模型提示的工具，以降低非AI专家开发复杂AI应用的门槛。
AI.motivation: 现有方法难以支持非AI专家通过链式调用LLM完成复杂任务，且缺乏对中间结果转换和调试的支持。
AI.method: 设计PromptChainer可视化界面，通过案例研究验证其在构建AI应用原型中的有效性。
AI.result: 四名用户案例研究表明PromptChainer可支持多种应用原型开发，但复杂任务扩展和低保真原型支持仍需探索。

search_text: PromptChainer: Chaining Large Language Model Prompts through Visual Programming PromptChainer：通过可视化编程链接大型语言模型提示 While LLMs can effectively help prototype single ML functionalities, many real-world applications involve complex tasks that cannot be easily handled via a single run of an LLM. Recent work has found that chaining multiple LLM runs together (with the output of one step being the input to the next) can help users accomplish these more complex tasks, and in a way that is perceived to be more transparent and controllable. However, it remains unknown what users need when authoring their own LLM chains -- a key step for lowering the barriers for non-AI-experts to prototype AI-infused applications. In this work, we explore the LLM chain authoring process. We conclude from pilot studies find that chaining requires careful scaffolding for transforming intermediate node outputs, as well as debugging the chain at multiple granularities; to help with these needs, we designed PromptChainer, an interactive interface for visually programming chains. Through case studies with four people, we show that PromptChainer supports building prototypes for a range of applications, and conclude with open questions on scaling chains to complex tasks, and supporting low-fi chain prototyping. 虽然 LLM 可以有效地帮助构建单一 ML 功能的原型，但许多实际应用程序涉及复杂的任务，无法通过单次运行 LLM 轻松处理。最近的工作发现，将多个 LLM 运行链接在一起（一个步骤的输出作为下一步的输入）可以帮助用户完成这些更复杂的任务，并且以一种被认为更加透明和可控的方式。然而，目前尚不清楚用户在编写自己的 LLM 链时需要什么，这是降低非人工智能专家制作人工智能应用程序原型的障碍的关键一步。在这项工作中，我们探索了 LLM 链创作过程。我们从试点研究中得出结论，发现链接需要仔细的脚手架来转换中间节点输出，以及在多个粒度上调试链；为了帮助满足这些需求，我们设计了 PromptChainer，一个用于可视化编程链的交互界面。通过四人的案例研究，我们表明 PromptChainer 支持为一系列应用程序构建原型，并以有关将链扩展到复杂任务和支持低保真链原型的开放性问题作为结论。 本文提出PromptChainer，一种通过可视化编程链式连接大语言模型提示的工具，以降低非AI专家开发复杂AI应用的门槛。 Tongshuang Wu,Ellen Jiang,Aaron Donsbach,Jeff Gray,Alejandra Molina,Michael Terry,Carrie J Cai cs.HC

authors_text: Yinpeng Dong,Shuyu Cheng,Tianyu Pang,Hang Su,Jun Zhu
title: Query-Efficient Black-box Adversarial Attacks Guided by a Transfer-based Prior
title_zh: 基于转移先验引导的高效查询黑盒对抗攻击
comment: Accepted by IEEE Transactions on Pattern Recognition and Machine Intelligence (TPAMI). The official version is at https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/9609659
comments: Accepted by IEEE Transactions on Pattern Recognition and Machine Intelligence (TPAMI). The official version is at https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/document/9609659
abstract: Adversarial attacks have been extensively studied in recent years since they can identify the vulnerability of deep learning models before deployed. In this paper, we consider the black-box adversarial setting, where the adversary needs to craft adversarial examples without access to the gradients of a target model. Previous methods attempted to approximate the true gradient either by using the transfer gradient of a surrogate white-box model or based on the feedback of model queries. However, the existing methods inevitably suffer from low attack success rates or poor query efficiency since it is difficult to estimate the gradient in a high-dimensional input space with limited information. To address these problems and improve black-box attacks, we propose two prior-guided random gradient-free (PRGF) algorithms based on biased sampling and gradient averaging, respectively. Our methods can take the advantage of a transfer-based prior given by the gradient of a surrogate model and the query information simultaneously. Through theoretical analyses, the transfer-based prior is appropriately integrated with model queries by an optimal coefficient in each method. Extensive experiments demonstrate that, in comparison with the alternative state-of-the-arts, both of our methods require much fewer queries to attack black-box models with higher success rates.
summary: Adversarial attacks have been extensively studied in recent years since they can identify the vulnerability of deep learning models before deployed. In this paper, we consider the black-box adversarial setting, where the adversary needs to craft adversarial examples without access to the gradients of a target model. Previous methods attempted to approximate the true gradient either by using the transfer gradient of a surrogate white-box model or based on the feedback of model queries. However, the existing methods inevitably suffer from low attack success rates or poor query efficiency since it is difficult to estimate the gradient in a high-dimensional input space with limited information. To address these problems and improve black-box attacks, we propose two prior-guided random gradient-free (PRGF) algorithms based on biased sampling and gradient averaging, respectively. Our methods can take the advantage of a transfer-based prior given by the gradient of a surrogate model and the query information simultaneously. Through theoretical analyses, the transfer-based prior is appropriately integrated with model queries by an optimal coefficient in each method. Extensive experiments demonstrate that, in comparison with the alternative state-of-the-arts, both of our methods require much fewer queries to attack black-box models with higher success rates.
abstract_zh: 近年来，对抗性攻击得到了广泛的研究，因为它们可以在部署之前识别深度学习模型的漏洞。在本文中，我们考虑黑盒对抗设置，其中对手需要在无法访问目标模型梯度的情况下制作对抗示例。以前的方法尝试通过使用代理白盒模型的传递梯度或基于模型查询的反馈来近似真实梯度。然而，现有的方法不可避免地存在攻击成功率低或查询效率差的问题，因为很难在信息有限的高维输入空间中估计梯度。为了解决这些问题并改进黑盒攻击，我们提出了两种分别基于偏差采样和梯度平均的先验引导随机无梯度（PRGF）算法。我们的方法可以利用由代理模型的梯度和查询信息同时给出的基于转移的先验。通过理论分析，基于转移的先验通过每种方法中的最佳系数与模型查询适当地集成。大量的实验表明，与其他最先进的技术相比，我们的两种方法都需要更少的查询来攻击黑盒模型，并且成功率更高。

AI.tldr: 本文提出两种基于迁移先验的高效黑盒对抗攻击算法，通过结合替代模型梯度和查询反馈提升攻击成功率并减少查询次数。
AI.motivation: 现有黑盒对抗攻击方法在高维输入空间中难以准确估计梯度，导致攻击成功率低或查询效率差。
AI.method: 提出两种PRGF算法：基于偏差采样的随机梯度-free方法和梯度平均方法，通过理论分析将迁移先验与查询信息以最优系数融合。
AI.result: 实验表明，相比现有最优方法，本文方法在更少查询次数下实现更高的黑盒模型攻击成功率。
AI.conclusion: 迁移先验与查询信息的有机结合有效解决了黑盒攻击中的梯度估计难题，显著提升了攻击效率和成功率。

search_text: Query-Efficient Black-box Adversarial Attacks Guided by a Transfer-based Prior 基于转移先验引导的高效查询黑盒对抗攻击 Adversarial attacks have been extensively studied in recent years since they can identify the vulnerability of deep learning models before deployed. In this paper, we consider the black-box adversarial setting, where the adversary needs to craft adversarial examples without access to the gradients of a target model. Previous methods attempted to approximate the true gradient either by using the transfer gradient of a surrogate white-box model or based on the feedback of model queries. However, the existing methods inevitably suffer from low attack success rates or poor query efficiency since it is difficult to estimate the gradient in a high-dimensional input space with limited information. To address these problems and improve black-box attacks, we propose two prior-guided random gradient-free (PRGF) algorithms based on biased sampling and gradient averaging, respectively. Our methods can take the advantage of a transfer-based prior given by the gradient of a surrogate model and the query information simultaneously. Through theoretical analyses, the transfer-based prior is appropriately integrated with model queries by an optimal coefficient in each method.
